one_time_description: Prior period adjustment: amortization overstated by $518,366 due to incorrect rate, and $607,353 in expenses reclassified from non-capital to capital (tangible capital assets)
economic_notes: COVID-19 impacts noted; Group deemed essential service and continued delivering services during pandemic. Nuxalk Development LP, Nuxalk Business Trust, and Bella Coola Hotel (0820770 B.C. Ltd.) are government business enterprises accounted for by modified equity method.
notes: Audit is qualified on two bases: (1) Social housing TCA amortized at CMHC-required rate (principal reduction) rather than useful life per PSAS; (2) GBE financial information not available for March 31, 2021 year-end, so equity method adjustments could not be verified. Prior period restatement increased 2020 accumulated surplus by $1,125,719. Obligations under capital lease of $383,649 for a fire pumper truck recorded in 2021 (nil in 2020). Three consolidated subsidiaries: Nuxalk Acwsalcmalslayc Academy of Learning Society, Nuxalk Nation Property Management, Nuxalk Nation Transition House Society. Three GBEs: Nuxalk Business Trust (100%), Nuxalk Development LP (99.9%), Bella Coola Hotel/0820770 B.C. Ltd. (100%). total_government_transfers calculated as ISC + FNHA + BC + Mid Coast Training Society + Fisheries and Oceans + Coast Opportunity Funds + FNESC + CMHC + Other contracts and government funding income = 17920240+3573309+2596319+208560+149457+284220+1734778+68544+3091327 = 31626754 less non-government OSR items (interest 20671, rental 340114, GBE income -409, admin revenue 723 = 361099); osr = 361099; however total_government_transfers adjusted to exclude Other contracts which may include non-government — reported as shown.
